finding: Loss of long-term depression (a synaptic rescaling mechanism) rather than gain-of-function predicts addiction-like loss of behavioral control. Animals unable to counteract drug-induced impairment in plasticity cannot modulate behavior by environmental contingencies.

Incidental tactile properties of objects nonconsciously influence higher-order social judgments through metaphor-based concept mapping: weight affects importance attributions, texture affects difficulty appraisals, hardness affects rigidity and stability judgments.

Syntactic incongruity in language elicits a positive-potential brain response (P600) at ~600 msec that is statistically indistinguishable from the response to harmonic incongruity in music, suggesting the P600 indexes domain-general structural integration difficulty rather than language-specific processing.

High-capability signals reduce hiring selection probability relative to sufficient-but-lower capability because managers infer lower commitment due to perceived misalignment with organizational goals and greater external job options; this effect is mediated by commitment perceptions and reversed when commitment is explicitly affirmed.

U.S. public clusters into three epistemological groups: traditional (prefer religion), modern (prefer science), and post-secular (favor both but selectively reject evolution/big bang). Post-secularism is not compromise but distinct worldview; all groups show reduced conflict avoidance on issues unrelated to life/creation.

Left-digit bias in price comparison is stronger under stimulus-based (simultaneous price display) than memory-based (recalled) evaluation because stimulus-based relies on perceptual digit-by-digit processing while memory-based relies on conceptual magnitude rounding; effect is enhanced by salient reference prices and more common in light category users.
